channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia deploys defence assets to Middle East amid regional conflict
item.description: The Australian Government has confirmed that defence personnel, military planes, and air-to-air missiles are being deployed to the Middle East in response to the escalating conflict involving the Unit...

item.title: Afghan Taliban Drone Attacks Injure Four, Do Not Hit Targets: ISPR Statement
item.description: On Friday evening, Pakistan's security forces intercepted two basic drones launched by the Afghan Taliban near Rawalpindi, successfully stopping them from hitting their intended targets using electron...

item.title: PM Modi criticises Congress over Assam issues ahead of state elections
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched a sharp criticism against the Congress party during his visit to poll-bound Assam, urging voters to hold the party accountable for allegedly favouring infiltrator...

item.title: Warwick Davis Finds Love Again After the Passing of His Wife
item.description: Warwick Davis, the celebrated actor known for roles in Harry Potter and Willow, has been seen publicly with his new partner, affectionately nicknamed 'Sponge'. The couple have been dating for over a y...

item.title: Robotics Lab Introduced at Siyifunile Secondary School in Dullstroom to Enhance Learning and Skills
item.description: In Dullstroom, Mpumalanga, Siyifunile Secondary School has recently launched a fully equipped robotics and coding laboratory, thanks to the support of the Shoprite Foundation and the Development Bank ...

item.title: How Segregation Shapes Food Culture in Delhi’s Muslim Neighbourhoods
item.description: In Delhi, the bustling food streets in Muslim localities like Jamia Nagar and Old Delhi are not just about culinary delight—they are shaped by a complex mix of social and spatial factors. The crowded ...

item.title: Holi Dispute in Delhi's Uttam Nagar Escalates Leading to Death
item.description: On the afternoon of March 4, during the Holi festival, a minor incident in Uttam Nagar, Delhi, escalated into a tragic confrontation. A child’s water balloon accidentally hit a woman from a neighbouri...

item.title: Study Suggests Sun Migrated from Milky Way’s Center with Other Sun-Like Stars
item.description: Researchers from Tokyo Metropolitan University and the National Astronomical Observatory of Japan have found evidence that our Sun likely migrated outward from the Milky Way's crowded central region a...

item.title: JD Vance Highlights Economic Improvements and Supports GOP Senate Candidate in North Carolina Visit
item.description: Vice President JD Vance visited Rocky Mount, North Carolina, on March 13, 2026, to promote recent economic achievements under the Trump administration and rally support for Republican candidates ahead...

item.title: PTI Announces Plans for Major Movement to Secure Imran Khan's Release After Eid-ul-Fitr
item.description: The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) has indicated that it will launch a massive movement for the release of its founder and former prime minister Imran Khan immediately after Eid-ul-Fitr. At a press co...

item.title: Conflict Between US-Israel and Iran Enters Prolonged Phase with Economic and Strategic Implications
item.description: On the 14th day of the US–Israel war against Iran, the conflict is moving into a prolonged and grinding stage, showing no signs of a decisive military victory for either side. A significant event was ...

item.title: Internal PDP Dispute Persists as Factions Struggle to Reconcile Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) dey experience heavy wahala as the Wike-backed faction and the Turaki-led group never fit agree on reconciliation. The Wike faction dey continue with state congresse...

item.title: Sri Lanka returns bodies of 84 Iranian sailors after naval attack
item.description: On Friday, Sri Lanka repatriated the remains of 84 Iranian sailors whose warship, IRIS Dena, was torpedoed by a US submarine on March 4 near Sri Lankan waters. The incident has escalated tensions from...

item.title: Canada’s oil production offers limited shield against global crisis caused by Strait of Hormuz disruption
item.description: Canada, as the world’s fourth-largest oil producer and exporter, benefits economically from surging global oil prices amid the U.S.-Israel war with Iran that has effectively blocked the Strait of Horm...

item.title: Ibadan Pastor Accused of Mismanaging Donations and Alleged Sexual Misconduct
item.description: Na Oyo State, Ibadan-based pastor, Kayode Amusan wey people sabi as Agbala Gabriel, don dey face serious accusations wey dey bother many people online. E don dey alleged say e misuse donations wey peo...

item.title: Senate Approves Use of AI Chatbots for Official Work by Staff
item.description: The U.S. Senate has officially approved the use of three artificial intelligence chatbots—Google's Gemini Chat, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and Microsoft Copilot—for staff to assist with routine official work. ...

item.title: Comelec reports smooth start to Antipolo 2nd district special elections with no incidents
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) reported a peaceful and incident-free start to the special elections in Antipolo City's second congressional district on March 14, 2026. Comelec Chairperson Georg...

item.title: Oman's Foreign Minister Warns of Broader Regional Implications of the Conflict with Iran
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly targeting Iran, has raised questions about the true objectives of the aggressors. Officially, the reasons cited have shifted over time, including ...

item.title: Aloria: Brisbane’s hotel restaurant with great food and views despite mixed messaging
item.description: Aloria, perched 23 floors above The Star casino in Brisbane, presents a confusing identity but delivers strong food and views. Opened in October 2024, the 120-seater has billed itself as a signature f...

item.title: Ekurhuleni and Eskom agree on repayment plan to prevent electricity interruptions
item.description: Residents and businesses in Ekurhuleni are set to continue receiving electricity without disruption after the City of Ekurhuleni and Eskom finalised a repayment agreement. On 5 March 2026, Eskom issue...
